$schema_description: Exchange Profile schema for Coinnect — factual reference data for money service providers

logo_url.description: URL to official logo (press kit or brand page)

regulated_in.description: List of jurisdictions or regulatory bodies

supported_currencies.description: Count or descriptive range of supported currencies

supported_countries.description: Count or descriptive range of supported countries

transfer_methods.description: Methods: bank, card, mobile money, crypto, cash pickup, etc.

fee_model.description: Brief factual description of how fees work

api_docs.description: Public API documentation URL, if available

coinnect_data_source.description: How Coinnect gets pricing data: CCXT order book, direct API, published fees

exchanges.name: Wise
exchanges.fee_model: Transparent variable fee (typically 0.33%–4.75% depending on currency pair) applied to the mid-market exchange rate with no markup. Fee shown upfront before confirmation.
exchanges.speed: 74% of transfers arrive in under 20 seconds; 95% within one day. Some currency pairs may take 1–6 days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2011 by Kristo Kaarmann and Taavet Hinrikus, who were personally frustrated by the cost of converting GBP and EUR.
Serves over 14.8 million customers, businesses, and banks, moving approximately $16 billion monthly.
Listed on the London Stock Exchange since July 2021.
Uses the mid-market exchange rate with no markup — one of the first major providers to do so.
Holds 65 regulatory licenses worldwide, operating in over 80 countries.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Public rates page (scraping) + open FX API fallback. No official partnership.

exchanges.name: Binance
regulated_in: UAE — ADGM (Recognised Investment Exchange, Recognised Clearing House, Broker-Dealer)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10% maker/taker, with tiered discounts based on volume and BNB holdings. Fiat deposit/withdrawal fees vary by method and currency.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits typically 1–3 business days via bank transfer, instant via card.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2017 by Changpeng Zhao; grew to become the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume.
Serves over 300 million registered users in more than 180 countries.
Supports over 1,300 cryptocurrencies and 50+ fiat currencies.
First crypto exchange to secure a global license under the ADGM framework (Abu Dhabi, 2026).
Operates Binance Academy, one of the largest free crypto education platforms.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.binance)

exchanges.name: Western Union
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state-level money transmitter licenses in all 50 states
exchanges.fee_model: Fees vary by corridor, amount, delivery method, and payment method. Online transfers tend to have lower fees than in-person agent transactions. Exchange rate includes a margin.
exchanges.speed: Cash pickup available within minutes in many corridors. Bank transfers typically 1–4 business days depending on destination.
interesting_facts: Founded in 1851, Western Union is one of the oldest financial services companies in the world.
Operates a network of approximately 455,000 agent locations across 200+ countries and territories.
Supports transfers in more than 130 currencies.
Originally a telegraph company — sent the first transcontinental telegram in 1861.
Publicly traded on the NYSE under ticker symbol WU.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees and exchange rate estimates (no public rate API for consumer transfers)

exchanges.name: Remitly
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
exchanges.fee_model: Per-transfer fee varies by corridor and delivery speed (Express or Economy). Typical fees range from $1.49–$3.99 per transfer. Exchange rate includes a margin that varies by corridor.
exchanges.speed: Express transfers can arrive in minutes (debit/credit card funding). Economy transfers typically 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2011 by Matt Oppenheimer, Josh Hug, and Shivaas Gulati.
Publicly traded on NASDAQ under ticker symbol RELY since September 2021.
Disbursement network includes 435,000+ cash pickup locations, ~4 billion bank accounts, ~1.1 billion mobile wallets, and 900 million cards.
Supports transfers from 17 sending countries to over 170 receiving countries.
Acquired Rewire (now Remitly for Immigrants) to serve long-term migrant financial needs.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees with open exchange rate API fallback (corridor-specific static estimates)

exchanges.name: OKX
regulated_in: UAE — VARA (Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority, Dubai)
EU — MiCA license (via OKCoin Europe Ltd, Malta)
Singapore — MAS Major Payment Institution license
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.08% maker / 0.10% taker, with tiered discounts based on volume and OKB holdings. P2P trading is fee-free for makers.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via P2P are typically instant; bank transfers 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2017 by Star Xu; originally launched as OKEx, rebranded to OKX in 2022.
Serves over 50 million users across 100+ countries.
Holds a MiCA-compliant license passported across all 30 EU/EEA member states.
Offers an integrated Web3 wallet with DEX aggregation, NFT marketplace, and DeFi access.
Official sponsor of Manchester City F.C. and the McLaren Formula 1 team.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.okx)

exchanges.name: Kraken
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), Wyoming SPDI charter (Special Purpose Depository Institution)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.16% maker / 0.26% taker for low volume, decreasing with higher 30-day volume. Fiat deposit/withdrawal fees vary by method (ACH free, wire $5–$35).
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. ACH deposits available in 1–5 business days; wire transfers 1–5 business days. Instant buy available via card.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2011 by Jesse Powell; one of the longest-operating crypto exchanges.
Holds a Wyoming Special Purpose Depository Institution (SPDI) charter — one of the first crypto-native bank charters in the US.
Supports 6 fiat currencies (USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, AUD, CHF) and 430+ cryptocurrencies.
Relocated global headquarters from San Francisco to Cheyenne, Wyoming in 2025.
Licensed as a CASP under MiCA in Ireland, enabling passporting across the EU/EEA.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.kraken)

exchanges.name: MoneyGram
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), licensed money transmitter in all 50 states
US — Georgia Department of Banking and Finance (NMLS #898432)
exchanges.fee_model: Fees vary by corridor, amount, delivery method, and payment method. The MoneyGram API provides a quote endpoint with send amount, receive amount, fees, and guaranteed FX rate.
exchanges.speed: Cash pickup available within minutes at 400,000+ agent locations. Bank deposits typically 1–3 business days. Mobile wallet transfers often same-day.
interesting_facts: Formed in 1988; one of the largest global money transfer networks alongside Western Union.
Operates approximately 400,000 agent locations in 200+ countries and territories.
Acquired by Madison Dearborn Partners in 2023, becoming a private company after years on NASDAQ.
Founding federated node operator on Midnight, a privacy-focused blockchain network.
Offers a public developer API for partners to integrate money transfer and crypto on/off-ramp services.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees (MoneyGram developer API available for partner integration)

exchanges.name: Payoneer
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
EU — Electronic Money Institution (Central Bank of Ireland)
Hong Kong — Money Service Operator license
US — OCC national trust bank charter application filed (Feb 2026, for PAYO Digital Bank, N.A.)
exchanges.fee_model: Cross-border payments in 150+ currencies. Balance withdrawals to local bank incur a fee of up to 2%. Currency conversion fee up to 2% above mid-market rate. Receiving payments from marketplaces is free.
exchanges.speed: Payments between Payoneer accounts are instant. Bank withdrawals typically 2–5 business days depending on destination country and bank.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2005; publicly traded on NASDAQ under ticker symbol PAYO since June 2021.
Serves businesses in over 200 countries, enabling cross-border payments in 150+ currencies.
Account balances available in USD, EUR, GBP, HKD, and JPY with local receiving accounts.
Partner of major global marketplaces including Amazon, Fiverr, Upwork, and Airbnb.
Filed application for a US national trust bank charter (PAYO Digital Bank, N.A.) in February 2026.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees (Payoneer API available for partner payout integration)

exchanges.name: Global66
regulated_in: Chile — CMF (Comision para el Mercado Financiero)
Peru — SBS (Superintendencia de Banca, Seguros y AFP)
Registered in each Latin American market where it operates
exchanges.fee_model: No explicit transfer fee for most international transfers. Revenue comes from a markup on the mid-market exchange rate. Domestic transfers may incur a small fee.
exchanges.speed: Most transfers completed within one business day. Instant transfers between Global66 users.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018 by Tomas Bercovich and Cristobal Forno in Santiago, Chile.
Operates in 6 Latin American countries (Chile, Colombia, Peru, Mexico, Argentina, Ecuador) with transfers to 80+ destinations.
Mobilized over US$3.7 billion in transfer volume across Latin America in 2025, a 157% year-over-year growth.
Offers a SmartCard (currently available in Chile, Colombia, and Peru) for international purchases and ATM withdrawals.
One of the fastest-growing fintechs in the Latin American cross-border payments space.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees (no public API available; exchange rate markup estimated from pricing page)

exchanges.name: Bitso
regulated_in: Mexico — CNBV (registered Financial Technology Institution under Fintech Law)
First crypto platform in Latin America licensed by the Gibraltar Financial Services Commission
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10%–0.60% maker/taker depending on 30-day volume tier. Fiat deposits via SPEI (Mexico) are free; other deposit methods vary.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. SPEI deposits (Mexico) typically within minutes during banking hours. International fiat 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2014 by Daniel Vogel, Ben Peters, and Pablo Gonzalez; the largest cryptocurrency exchange in Latin America.
First crypto platform in Latin America to receive a DLT license from the Gibraltar Financial Services Commission.
Registered as a Financial Technology Institution (ITF) under Mexico's Fintech Law, supervised by the CNBV.
Supports fiat currencies MXN, ARS, COP, and BRL — enabling localized trading across key Latin American markets.
Offers Bitso Business with APIs for developers to access crypto liquidity and LatAm local payment rails.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Direct API (live order book via Bitso REST API — not in CCXT, custom adapter)

exchanges.name: Coinbase
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses in most states
EU — MiFID-regulated entity for crypto futures across 26 European countries
exchanges.fee_model: Coinbase Advanced spot trading fees start at 0.60% maker / 1.20% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume. Advanced tiers reach 0.00% maker / 0.05% taker at $500M+ volume.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. ACH deposits typically 3–5 business days for settlement. Wire transfers 1–3 business days. Instant buy available via debit card.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2012 by Brian Armstrong and Fred Ehrsam.
Became the first major cryptocurrency exchange to go public, listing on NASDAQ under ticker symbol COIN in April 2021.
Operates as a remote-first company, serving over 100 million verified users in 177 countries.
Launched Base, a Layer 2 blockchain built on the OP Stack, in August 2023.
Offers crypto futures trading across 26 European countries through its MiFID-regulated entity.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.coinbase)

exchanges.name: Gemini
regulated_in: US — NYDFS (New York Trust Company, BitLicense)
EU — MiCA license via Malta Financial Services Authority (MFSA)
exchanges.fee_model: ActiveTrader spot fees start at 0.20% maker / 0.40% taker for low volume, decreasing with higher 30-day volume. Maker rebate of -0.01% available at $500M+ volume tier.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. ACH deposits available within 4–5 business days. Wire transfers typically 1–3 business days. Instant buy via debit card.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2014 by Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss; went live in October 2015.
Regulated as a New York Trust Company, one of the earliest crypto-native entities to hold this charter.
Obtained a MiCA license from the Malta Financial Services Authority in August 2025, enabling operation across all 27 EU member states.
Went public on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under ticker symbol GEMI in September 2025.
Issued the Gemini Dollar (GUSD), one of the first regulated stablecoins backed 1:1 by USD.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.gemini)

exchanges.name: Bybit
regulated_in: UAE — SCA (Virtual Asset Platform Operator License)
Austria — FMA (MiCA CASP authorization, EEA passporting)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10% maker / 0.10% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume. Supports up to 125x leverage on major crypto futures contracts.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via P2P typically instant; bank transfers 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018 by Ben Zhou, a former forex broker executive.
Relocated global headquarters from Singapore to Dubai in 2022.
Lists over 2,500 cryptocurrencies, making it one of the largest exchanges by asset count.
Received MiCA CASP authorization from Austria's FMA, enabling regulated services across the EEA.
Available in over 160 countries, serving a global user base focused on derivatives and spot trading.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.bybit)

exchanges.name: KuCoin
regulated_in: Austria — FMA (MiCA CASP authorization, EEA passporting)
Turks and Caicos Islands — re-domiciled operating entity (2025)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10% maker / 0.10% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume and KCS holdings. Futures trading fees from 0.02% maker / 0.06% taker.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via P2P or third-party partners typically instant to 1 business day.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2017 by Chun Gan, Ke Tang, and Johnny Lyu.
Serves users in over 200 countries with support for 700+ cryptocurrencies.
Received MiCA CASP authorization from Austria's FMA for compliant services across the EEA.
Known for early listing of emerging tokens, earning the nickname 'The People's Exchange.'
Offers KuCoin Earn, lending, and bot trading alongside standard spot and futures markets.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.kucoin)

exchanges.name: Gate.io
regulated_in: Malta — MFSA (MiCA license and PSD2 Payment Institution license)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.20% maker / 0.20% taker at base tier, with tiered discounts based on 30-day volume and GT (GateToken) holdings.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via third-party payment providers typically instant to 1 business day.
interesting_facts: Originally launched as Bter.com in 2013, rebranded to Gate.io in 2017.
Lists over 3,500 tokens, one of the highest asset counts among global exchanges.
Serves over 46 million users across 150+ countries.
Obtained a PSD2 Payment Institution license from Malta's MFSA in February 2026.
Holds regulatory approvals in 8+ jurisdictions including Malta, Dubai, Japan, and Gibraltar.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.gateio)

exchanges.name: Bitget
regulated_in: El Salvador — BCR (Bitcoin Services Provider) and CNAD (Digital Asset Service Provider)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10% maker / 0.10% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume and BGB holdings. Copy trading and futures trading available with separate fee schedules.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via card or third-party providers typically instant.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018; one of the leading copy trading platforms in the cryptocurrency space.
Supports over 1,300 cryptocurrencies with 900+ spot trading pairs.
Registered as a VASP in multiple European and Latin American jurisdictions.
Holds both a Bitcoin Services Provider and Digital Asset Service Provider license in El Salvador.
Available in over 150 countries with a focus on derivatives and social trading features.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.bitget)

exchanges.name: MEXC
regulated_in: Previously operated under limited offshore regulation
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading offers 0% maker fees as a promotional rate; taker fees start at 0.05%. Futures fees start at 0.00% maker / 0.02% taker.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via third-party providers typically instant to same-day.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018; lists over 3,000 cryptocurrencies, one of the widest selections globally.
Known for rapid new token listings, often among the first exchanges to list emerging projects.
Obtained FCA authorization in the UK in 2025, expanding its regulated presence.
Serves users in over 170 countries with support for multiple fiat on-ramp partners.
Offers zero maker fees on spot trading as a competitive differentiator.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.mexc)

exchanges.name: HTX
regulated_in: Offices in Hong Kong, South Korea, Japan, and the United States
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.20% maker / 0.20% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume and HT holdings. Futures trading fees from 0.02% maker / 0.04% taker.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via P2P or card typically instant to same-day.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2013 by Leon Li; originally launched as Huobi, rebranded to HTX in September 2023.
Serves over 45 million registered users across 160+ countries.
Supports over 700 digital currencies and tokens for spot and derivatives trading.
Holds a Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) license in Dubai.
One of the earliest cryptocurrency exchanges founded in China, later relocating internationally.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.htx)

exchanges.name: Crypto.com
regulated_in: Australia — ASIC (Digital Currency Exchange and Financial Services license)
Hong Kong — SFC (Virtual Asset Trading Platform license)
South Korea — VASP and Electronic Financial Transaction Act
Dubai — VARA (Virtual Assets Service Provider license)
Brazil — Payment Institution license and COAF registration
Canada — FINTRAC and provincial securities registrations
UK — FCA (registered crypto asset service provider)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.075% maker / 0.075% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume and CRO staking. Crypto.com Visa Card offers cashback on spending.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. Fiat deposits via bank transfer typically 1–3 business days. Instant buy via card available.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2016 by Bobby Bao, Gary Or, Kris Marszalek, and Rafael Melo.
Originally based in Hong Kong, relocated headquarters to Singapore in 2021.
Holds licenses in 10+ jurisdictions spanning Asia-Pacific, Middle East, Americas, and Europe.
Offers the Crypto.com Visa Card with tiered cashback rewards based on CRO staking.
Secured naming rights for the former Staples Center in Los Angeles, now Crypto.com Arena, in a 20-year deal.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.cryptocom)

exchanges.name: Luno
regulated_in: Malaysia — Securities Commission (registered Digital Asset Exchange)
exchanges.fee_model: Instant buy/sell fee of approximately 0.75%–1.5% depending on market conditions. Exchange (limit orders) maker fee 0%, taker fee 0.10% at base tier, decreasing with volume.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. Fiat deposits vary by country: South Africa (EFT) typically same-day; bank transfers in other markets 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2013 as BitX by Marcus Swanepoel and Timothy Stranex; rebranded to Luno in 2016.
Serves over 11 million users across 40+ countries in Africa, Southeast Asia, and Europe.
Regulated by the Securities Commission Malaysia, one of the first crypto exchanges to receive this approval.
Offices in London, Cape Town, and Singapore.
Focused on emerging markets, particularly South Africa, Nigeria, Malaysia, and Indonesia.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.luno)

exchanges.name: Bitstamp
regulated_in: Luxembourg — CSSF (Payment Institution license, passported across EU)
EU — MiFID II (Multilateral Trading Facility approval)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.30% maker / 0.40% taker for volumes under $10,000/month, decreasing with higher 30-day volume. Stablecoin pairs have reduced fees.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. SEPA deposits typically 1–3 business days. Wire transfers 2–5 business days. Card purchases instant.
interesting_facts: Founded in August 2011 by Nejc Kodric and Damijan Merlak in Slovenia; one of the longest-operating crypto exchanges.
First crypto exchange to receive a Payment Institution license in the EU (Luxembourg, 2016).
Holds over 50 global licenses and registrations, including a BitLicense in New York.
Approved as a Multilateral Trading Facility (MTF) under MiFID II.
Offices in Luxembourg, Ljubljana, Singapore, London, and the USA.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.bitstamp)

exchanges.name: BtcTurk
regulated_in: Turkey — SPK (Capital Markets Board of Turkey, authorized)
Turkey — compliance with AML/KYC requirements as mandated by Turkish financial authorities
Founding member of AKDEP (Association of Cryptocurrency Platforms, self-regulatory organization)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees vary by 30-day volume tier. TRY trading pairs available. Fees decrease with higher trading volume. Fiat deposits via Turkish bank transfer are typically free.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. TRY deposits via Turkish bank transfer typically processed same-day during banking hours.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2013; the first cryptocurrency trading platform in Turkey.
Authorized under SPK (Capital Markets Board of Turkey) licensing.
Founding member of AKDEP, a self-regulatory organization for Turkish crypto platforms established in 2021.
Supports 150+ cryptocurrencies traded against the Turkish lira (TRY).
Deeply integrated with Turkish banking infrastructure, offering localized services for the Turkish market.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.btcturk)

exchanges.name: Bithumb
regulated_in: South Korea — FIU (registered Virtual Asset Service Provider)
South Korea — compliance with KYC/AML regulations under Korean law
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.25% maker / 0.25% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume. KRW deposit and withdrawal fees apply.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. KRW deposits via Korean bank transfer typically processed within minutes during banking hours.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2014; originally named Xcoin, rebranded to Bithumb in 2015.
One of the largest cryptocurrency exchanges in South Korea by trading volume.
Headquartered in the Gangnam district of Seoul, a major financial hub.
Supports over 400 cryptocurrencies traded against the Korean won (KRW).
Registered as a Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) with South Korea's Financial Intelligence Unit.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.bithumb)

exchanges.name: bitFlyer
regulated_in: Japan — FSA (Japan Financial Services Agency, registered Virtual Currency Exchange Service)
EU — licensed to operate across EU/EEA member states
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees vary by market. Japanese market: 0.01%–0.15% depending on pair and volume. US and EU markets have separate fee schedules. Lightning FX (futures) trading available.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. JPY deposits via Japanese bank transfer typically same-day. International wire transfers 1–5 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2014 by Yuzo Kano, a former Goldman Sachs derivatives and bonds trader.
One of the largest cryptocurrency exchanges in Japan by trading volume.
Licensed to operate in Japan, the US (including New York), and Europe — one of the few exchanges with licenses in all three regions.
Supports 39 cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Litecoin.
Operates bitFlyer Lightning, a professional trading platform for spot and derivatives.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.bitflyer)

exchanges.name: Independent Reserve
regulated_in: Singapore — MAS (approved for digital payment token services)
Acquired by IG Group Holdings plc in January 2026
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.50% for volumes under AUD 100,000/month, decreasing with higher volume tiers. AUD deposits via OSKO/PayID are free.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. AUD deposits via OSKO typically instant. International bank transfers 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2013 by Adrian Przelozny, Adam Tepper, and Lasanka Perera.
Registered with AUSTRAC as a Digital Currency Exchange and approved by MAS in Singapore.
Acquired by IG Group Holdings plc in January 2026, with the transaction completing after MAS regulatory approval.
Serves users primarily in Australia, Singapore, New Zealand, and expanding to the UAE.
One of the longest-operating cryptocurrency exchanges in Australia.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.independentreserve)

exchanges.name: VALR
regulated_in: South Africa — FSCA (Category I and Category II Crypto Asset Service Provider licenses)
Dubai — VARA (initial approval for virtual asset services)
EU — approved to offer services in European markets
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10% maker / 0.25% taker, decreasing with 30-day volume. ZAR deposits via EFT are free. OTC desk available for large orders.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. ZAR deposits via EFT typically processed same-day. International bank transfers 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018; Africa's largest crypto exchange by trade volume.
Licensed by South Africa's FSCA with both Category I and Category II Crypto Asset Service Provider licenses.
Serves over 1 million users globally, including 1,100+ corporate and institutional clients.
Received initial approval from Dubai's VARA for virtual asset services.
Backed by investors including Pantera Capital, Coinbase Ventures, and Distributed Global.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.valr)

exchanges.name: CoinDCX
regulated_in: India — compliance with AML/CFT guidelines and KYC requirements
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10% maker / 0.10% taker at base tier, decreasing with 30-day volume. INR deposits via UPI and bank transfer available.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. INR deposits via UPI typically instant. Bank transfers typically same-day during banking hours.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018 by Sumit Gupta and Neeraj Khandelwal.
India's largest crypto exchange, serving over 15 million registered users.
Registered with FIU-IND as a reporting entity for AML/CFT compliance.
Supports 500+ cryptocurrencies with INR trading pairs.
Backed by investors including Pantera Capital, Steadview Capital, and Coinbase Ventures.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.coindcx)

exchanges.name: WazirX
regulated_in: India — member of Bharat Web3 Association (BWA)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees of 0.20% maker / 0.20% taker. WRX token holders receive fee discounts. P2P trading available for INR on/off-ramp.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. INR deposits via UPI or bank transfer typically processed same-day.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2017 by Nischal Shetty, Sameer Mhatre, and Siddharth Menon.
One of India's most widely used cryptocurrency exchanges, serving over 16 million users.
Registered with FIU-IND as a reporting entity and member of the Bharat Web3 Association.
Offers P2P trading for INR deposits and withdrawals alongside standard exchange features.
Supports 200+ cryptocurrencies with INR and USDT trading pairs.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.wazirx)

exchanges.name: SatoshiTango
regulated_in: Argentina — registered with local financial authorities
Operates across 33 countries in Latin America and beyond
exchanges.fee_model: Buy/sell spread varies by cryptocurrency and payment method. Supports purchases in ARS, USD, BRL, and EUR. Fee structure includes a spread on the exchange rate.
exchanges.speed: Crypto purchases processed within minutes. ARS deposits via bank transfer typically same-day during banking hours.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2014 in Buenos Aires; one of the pioneering cryptocurrency platforms in Argentina.
Available in 33 countries, primarily serving Latin American users.
Supports purchases in multiple fiat currencies including Argentine pesos, US dollars, Brazilian reals, and euros.
Offers a range of cryptocurrencies including BTC, ETH, LTC, XRP, USDT, USDC, and ADA.
Known for supporting various local payment methods across Latin America.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.satoshitango)

exchanges.name: Buda.com
regulated_in: Chile — registered with local financial authorities
Colombia — registered with local financial authorities
Peru — registered with local financial authorities
Argentina — registered with local financial authorities
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees vary by 30-day volume tier, starting at 0.80% maker / 0.80% taker for low volume. Fiat deposits via local bank transfer are typically free.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. Local fiat deposits typically processed same-day during banking hours.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2015 by Guillermo Torrealba and Alejandro Beltran; formerly known as SurBTC.
Serves four Latin American markets: Chile, Colombia, Peru, and Argentina.
Supports Bitcoin, Ether, Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash, and USDC with local fiat pairs.
One of the first cryptocurrency exchanges in Chile, pioneering crypto adoption in the region.
Focuses on compliance with local regulatory requirements across each Latin American market it serves.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.buda)

exchanges.name: Ria Money Transfer
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
Nigeria — Central Bank of Nigeria (approved international money transfer operator)
Subsidiary of Euronet Worldwide, Inc. (NASDAQ: EEFT)
exchanges.fee_model: Transfer fees range from $0.99–$9 depending on corridor, amount, and payment/delivery method. Exchange rate includes a markup of 0.25%–3.5% above mid-market rate. Fees shown upfront before confirmation.
exchanges.speed: Cash pickup available within minutes at 500,000+ locations. Bank transfers typically 1–4 business days depending on destination. Mobile wallet transfers often same-day.
interesting_facts: Founded in 1987, growing from a single storefront in New York City to a global network.
Subsidiary of Euronet Worldwide, Inc. (NASDAQ: EEFT) since 2007.
Operates a network of over 500,000 agent locations across 160+ countries.
One of the largest money transfer companies worldwide by network size.
Approved by the Central Bank of Nigeria as an international money transfer operator for diaspora remittances.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fee estimates and exchange rate markup data (no public rate API)

exchanges.name: WorldRemit
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (registered), state money transmitter licenses
exchanges.fee_model: Per-transfer fee varies by corridor, delivery method, and payment method. Exchange rate includes a margin. Fees shown upfront in the app or website before confirmation.
exchanges.speed: Mobile money transfers often arrive within minutes. Bank transfers typically 1–3 business days. Cash pickup available within minutes at partner locations.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2010 by Ismail Ahmed, Catherine Wines, and Richard Igoe.
Part of Zepz (formerly WorldRemit Group), which also operates Sendwave.
Serves users in 130+ countries with 70+ supported currencies.
Digital-first remittance service supporting mobile wallet, bank transfer, cash pickup, and airtime top-up.
Regional offices in the US, Poland, Canada, Australia, and multiple African and Asian countries.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fee estimates with open FX rate fallback (corridor-specific static estimates)

exchanges.name: Sendwave
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
exchanges.fee_model: No upfront transfer fee on most corridors. Revenue comes from a margin on the exchange rate. Fees are built into the rate shown before confirmation.
exchanges.speed: Transfers to mobile wallets in Africa typically arrive within seconds to minutes. Bank transfers may take 1–2 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2014; acquired by WorldRemit (now Zepz) in 2021.
Specializes in instant money transfers from North America, Europe, and Asia to Africa and South Asia.
Supports transfers to mobile wallets and bank accounts in countries including Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Ghana, Nigeria, Senegal, and Bangladesh.
No upfront transfer fees on most corridors — one of the simplest pricing models in the remittance space.
Focused on serving the diaspora community with fast, affordable mobile-first transfers.

exchanges.name: Xoom
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
Subsidiary of PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL)
Regulated under PayPal's global licenses across multiple jurisdictions
exchanges.fee_model: Transfer fees vary by corridor, delivery method, and payment method (typically $0–$4.99 per transfer). Exchange rate includes a margin above mid-market rate. PayPal balance funding often has lowest fees.
exchanges.speed: Cash pickup and mobile wallet transfers available within minutes. Bank deposits typically 1–4 business days depending on destination.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2001 by Alan Braverman and Kevin Hartz.
Acquired by PayPal in 2015 for $890 million.
Supports money transfers to over 160 countries, with bill pay and mobile reload services in select markets.
Deeply integrated with PayPal, allowing users to fund transfers from PayPal balance.
Strong presence in Mexico, India, the Philippines, China, and Brazil.

regulated_in: EU — Central Bank of Ireland (authorized Electronic Money Institution)
exchanges.fee_model: Flat fee per transfer (typically £1/$2 or equivalent) on most corridors. Exchange rate includes a margin above mid-market rate. Fee shown upfront before confirmation.
exchanges.speed: Card-to-card and mobile wallet transfers typically arrive within minutes to same-day. Bank transfers 1–3 business days depending on destination.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2017; headquartered in London with global reach to 170+ countries.
Authorized and regulated by the FCA (UK) and Central Bank of Ireland.
Operates on a simple flat-fee model for most corridors.
Supports sending from 49 countries and receiving in 170+ countries.
Offers card-to-card transfers as a distinctive feature alongside bank and mobile wallet delivery.

exchanges.name: TransferGo
regulated_in: UK — FCA (authorized payment institution, reference 600886), supervised by HMRC
Lithuania — Bank of Lithuania (authorized Electronic Money Institution)
exchanges.fee_model: Transfer fees vary by speed tier: NOW (instant), FAST (hours), and STANDARD (1–3 days). Fees start from £0.99/€0.99. Exchange rate includes a small margin.
exchanges.speed: NOW transfers arrive within 30 minutes. FAST transfers within a few hours. STANDARD transfers 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2012 by Daumantas Dvilinskas, Justinas Lasevicius, and Edvinas Sershniovas.
Serves over 8 million customers in more than 160 countries.
Offers three speed tiers (NOW, FAST, STANDARD) allowing users to choose between speed and cost.
Offices in the UK, Lithuania, Germany, Poland, and Turkey.
Authorized as a payment institution by the FCA and the Bank of Lithuania.

Authorized as a Payments Institution by the FCA and the National Bank of Belgium.
Expanded to the US, Canada, and Australia in 2024.
Positions itself as one of the most cost-effective options for large international transfers.

exchanges.name: Yellow Card
regulated_in: South Africa — FSCA (Crypto Asset Service Provider license)
exchanges.fee_model: Fees vary by transaction type and country. Stablecoin on/off-ramp fees apply. B2B infrastructure pricing available for business partners.

exchanges.name: Strike
regulated_in: US — NYDFS (BitLicense, obtained March 2026), FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
El Salvador — licensed under digital assets regulatory framework
UK — registered for bitcoin and crypto services
exchanges.fee_model: No fees on peer-to-peer Bitcoin payments over Lightning Network. Spread on buy/sell orders. ACH deposits are free. Competitive rates for cross-border payments via Bitcoin rails.
exchanges.speed: Lightning Network payments settle in seconds. On-chain Bitcoin transactions confirmed in minutes. ACH deposits 1–3 business days. Cross-border payments via Lightning near-instant.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018 by Jack Mallers; focused on Bitcoin and Lightning Network payments.
Secured a New York BitLicense in March 2026, enabling bitcoin financial services for New York residents.
Available in 100+ countries and territories, including the US, UK, EU, Latin America, and Africa.
Uses the Bitcoin Lightning Network for near-instant, low-cost cross-border payments.
One of the first companies licensed under El Salvador's digital assets regulatory framework.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.strike)
